final-inventory-operations-launch.label: Final Capstone: Inventory Operations Launch
final-inventory-operations-launch.summary: Build a two-table makers-market inventory database through six cumulative schema and data-management steps.

ci_final_capstone_step6.title: Remove the Test Event and Finalize

ci_final_capstone_step1.title: Build the Product Table
ci_final_capstone_step1.prompt: The neighborhood makers market is replacing its launch spreadsheet with a real product catalog. In `schema.sql`, create `products` with these exact columns: `id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY`; `sku TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE`; `name TEXT NOT NULL`; `category TEXT NOT NULL`; `price REAL NOT NULL`; `status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft'`; and optional `notes TEXT`. Leave `seed.sql` and `query.sql` unchanged for now.
ci_final_capstone_step1.hint: This is the first project step. Build a clean starting version, then run it before moving on.

help.concept: The final capstone is progressive. Each step starts from the previous working solution and adds one focused feature.
help.hint_1: This is the first project step. Build a clean starting version, then run it before moving on.
help.hint_2: Click Run query and compare the results table with the expected result.

ci_final_capstone_step1.solutionCode:
CREATE TABLE products (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    sku TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    category TEXT NOT NULL,
    price REAL NOT NULL,
    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft',
    notes TEXT NULL
);

schema_sql.content:
CREATE TABLE products (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    sku TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    category TEXT NOT NULL,
    price REAL NOT NULL,
    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft',
    notes TEXT NULL
);

ci_final_capstone_step2.title: Build the Stock Event Table
ci_final_capstone_step2.prompt: The market also needs an auditable history of stock receipts and adjustments. Keep the completed `products` table, then add `stock_events` in `schema.sql` with `id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY`, `product_id INTEGER NOT NULL`, `quantity_change INTEGER NOT NULL`, `event_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'receipt'`, optional `note TEXT`, and `FOREIGN KEY (product_id) REFERENCES products(id)`.
ci_final_capstone_step2.hint: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

help.concept: The final capstone is progressive. Each step starts from the previous working solution and adds one focused feature.
help.hint_1: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.
help.hint_2: Run the previous working code first, then make only the focused change for this step.

ci_final_capstone_step3.title: Load the Approved Products
ci_final_capstone_step3.prompt: The launch committee approved three catalog rows. In `seed.sql`, use one multi-row `INSERT` into `products` with columns `sku`, `name`, `category`, `price`, and `notes`: `SKU001 / Product A / Category 1 / 19.99 / NULL`; `SKU002 / Product B / Category 2 / 29.99 / 'Special edition'`; and `SKU003 / Product C / Category 3 / 39.99 / NULL`. Omit `status` so every row receives the default `draft` value.
ci_final_capstone_step3.hint: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

ci_final_capstone_step4.title: Load the Opening Stock Events
ci_final_capstone_step4.prompt: The opening inventory counts are ready. Keep the three product rows, then in `seed.sql` insert these `stock_events` using `product_id`, `quantity_change`, `event_type`, and `note`: product `1`, `100`, `receipt`, `Initial stock`; product `2`, `50`, `receipt`, `Restock`; and product `3`, `30`, `receipt`, `New shipment`.
ci_final_capstone_step4.hint: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

ci_final_capstone_step5.title: Apply the Launch Correction
ci_final_capstone_step5.prompt: A final pricing review changed the launch record for product `id = 1`. In `query.sql`, update only that product so `price = 24.99` and `status = 'active'`. Preserve the completed schema and seed data.
ci_final_capstone_step5.hint: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

ci_final_capstone_step6.title: Remove the Test Event and Finalize
ci_final_capstone_step6.prompt: Quality assurance found one placeholder stock event that must not reach production. Keep the price-and-status correction from the previous step, then in `query.sql` delete only the row from `stock_events` whose `note = 'Test event'`. Do not broaden the `WHERE` clause.
ci_final_capstone_step6.hint: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

help.concept: The final capstone is progressive. Each step starts from the previous working solution and adds one focused feature.
help.hint_1: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

SET
    price = 24.99,
    status = 'active'
WHERE
    id = 1;
-- Capstone step 6: Remove the Test Event and Finalize
-- Keep the working code above from the previous step.
-- Next, Delete the test event from `stock_events` in `query.sql` using a narrow WHERE clause
-- Add only the focused change for this step inside the existing work.
ci_final_capstone_step6.solutionCode:
UPDATE products
SET
    price = 24.99,
    status = 'active'
WHERE
    id = 1;

DELETE FROM stock_events
WHERE
    note = 'Test event';

sketch-1.title: Inventory Operations Launch Project
sketch-1.bodyMarkdown:
A neighborhood makers market is replacing its launch spreadsheet with a small operational database. You are the SQL data management specialist responsible for preparing a production-ready three-file workspace.

The launch has six concrete stages: define the `products` table, define the related `stock_events` table, load three approved products, load three opening stock events, correct product `1`, and remove one placeholder event. Each step edits the file where that work belongs: `schema.sql`, `seed.sql`, or `query.sql`.

Your final deliverable must preserve the complete schema and seed history while leaving product `1` priced at `24.99` with status `active` and removing only the stock event whose note is `Test event`.
